title: Prolific Instructors
description: You are given three tables: instructors, courses, and a teaches table linking them. Find names of instructors who teach more than 1 course, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
instructors(id, name, dept)
courses(id, title, credits)
teaches(id, instructor_id, course_id)
outputFormat: One instructor name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use JOIN and GROUP BY. Use HAVING COUNT(*) > 1. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT i.name FROM instructors i JOIN teaches t ON i.id = t.instructor_id GROUP BY i.id, i.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY i.name;
hint: Join instructors with teaches, GROUP BY instructor, and filter with HAVING COUNT(*) > 1.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE instructors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept TEXT); CREATE TABLE courses (id INTEGER, title TEXT, credits INTEGER); CREATE TABLE teaches (id INTEGER, instructor_id INTEGER, course_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO instructors VALUES (1,'Alice','Math'),(2,'Bob','CS'),(3,'Charlie','Physics'); INSERT INTO courses VALUES (1,'Calculus',4),(2,'Algebra',3),(3,'CS101',3),(4,'CS201',3),(5,'Mechanics',4); INSERT INTO teaches VALUES (1,1,1),(2,1,2),(3,2,3),(4,2,4),(5,3,5);

CREATE TABLE instructors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept TEXT); CREATE TABLE courses (id INTEGER, title TEXT, credits INTEGER); CREATE TABLE teaches (id INTEGER, instructor_id INTEGER, course_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO instructors VALUES (1,'Diana','Biology'),(2,'Eve','Chemistry'),(3,'Frank','History'); INSERT INTO courses VALUES (1,'Cell Biology',3),(2,'Genetics',4),(3,'Organic Chem',4),(4,'History 101',3),(5,'Modern History',3); INSERT INTO teaches VALUES (1,1,1),(2,1,2),(3,2,3),(4,3,4),(5,3,5);

CREATE TABLE instructors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept TEXT); CREATE TABLE courses (id INTEGER, title TEXT, credits INTEGER); CREATE TABLE teaches (id INTEGER, instructor_id INTEGER, course_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO instructors VALUES (1,'Grace','Art'),(2,'Hank','Music'); INSERT INTO courses VALUES (1,'Painting',2),(2,'Drawing',2),(3,'Piano',3),(4,'Violin',3); INSERT INTO teaches VALUES (1,1,1),(2,2,3);

CREATE TABLE instructors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept TEXT); CREATE TABLE courses (id INTEGER, title TEXT, credits INTEGER); CREATE TABLE teaches (id INTEGER, instructor_id INTEGER, course_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO instructors VALUES (1,'Iris','CS'),(2,'Jack','Math'),(3,'Kate','Physics'); INSERT INTO courses VALUES (1,'Algo',3),(2,'Data Structures',3),(3,'Linear Algebra',3),(4,'Statistics',3),(5,'Mechanics',4),(6,'Thermodynamics',4); INSERT INTO teaches VALUES (1,1,1),(2,1,2),(3,2,3),(4,2,4),(5,3,5),(6,3,6);

CREATE TABLE instructors (id INTEGER, name TEXT, dept TEXT); CREATE TABLE courses (id INTEGER, title TEXT, credits INTEGER); CREATE TABLE teaches (id INTEGER, instructor_id INTEGER, course_id INTEGER); INSERT INTO instructors VALUES (1,'Leo','Economics'),(2,'Mia','Sociology'); INSERT INTO courses VALUES (1,'Micro',3),(2,'Macro',3),(3,'Soc 101',3),(4,'Urban',3); INSERT INTO teaches VALUES (1,1,1),(2,1,2),(3,1,3),(4,2,4);
